contracts.userOutcome: First-block aliases and page properties remain editable, persist, and resolve their page.

acceptableVariations: Autocomplete ranking and editor layout may evolve while the saved alias still resolves the page.
nonRequirements: Exact picker dimensions, animation, or DOM shape.

contracts.userOutcome: Embedded blocks keep local disclosure, keyboard navigation, deletion, and caret ownership in the visible embed.

acceptableVariations: Embed styling and control placement may evolve without changing the focused editing surface.
nonRequirements: Exact indentation pixels or disclosure-icon artwork.

contracts.userOutcome: Copied block references refresh visible reference counts and text after saved source changes.

acceptableVariations: Refresh timing may vary within the completed save lifecycle.
nonRequirements: Exact badge typography or animation.

contracts.userOutcome: Escape and arrow keys transition between editing and block selection without losing the live selection.

acceptableVariations: Selection affordance styling may evolve while keyboard ownership remains intact.
nonRequirements: Exact selection highlight color or transition timing.

contracts.userOutcome: The E2E runner can discover native-driver capabilities and retain browser-specific evidence.

acceptableVariations: Driver feature detection may vary by supported platform and browser version.
nonRequirements: Any end-user product workflow by itself.

contracts.userOutcome: Quick Capture activates, accepts first-show typing, saves the journal entry, and supports a safe second-instance handoff.

acceptableVariations: Native focus timing and frame decoration may vary by window manager.
nonRequirements: Exact window position, border pixels, or focus-animation timing.

contracts.userOutcome: Arrow navigation preserves a usable caret across wrapped blocks and journal entries.

acceptableVariations: Line wrapping may differ with fonts and viewport width while visual-row caret navigation remains correct.
nonRequirements: Exact glyph metrics or line height.

contracts.userOutcome: A click maps to the intended source caret, including after trailing links and inside inline code.

acceptableVariations: Layout and coordinates may differ, but mapping a click to the intended source caret is blocking.
nonRequirements: Exact text rasterization, font metrics, or pointer coordinate values.

contracts.userOutcome: Empty or invalid friendly searches open independent virtual workspaces without querying or writing the graph.

acceptableVariations: Empty-state wording and tab presentation may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact modal geometry or tab animation.

contracts.userOutcome: An approved external asset directory is read and written through its exact canonical graph and target identity.
contracts.authority: Tine external-asset consent and canonical-path contract

acceptableVariations: The native file-picker implementation and media presentation may vary after consent is established.
nonRequirements: Exact dialog appearance or host file-manager behavior.

contracts.userOutcome: Custom journal filename and title formats load and render the corresponding journal feed entries.

acceptableVariations: Locale rendering and feed layout may vary while configured dates remain discoverable.
nonRequirements: Exact locale punctuation or date-label spacing.

contracts.userOutcome: Future journals stay out of the feed while remaining directly reachable without changing their file bytes.

acceptableVariations: Feed ordering and date formatting may evolve around the inclusion boundary.
nonRequirements: Exact feed row height or clock display.

contracts.userOutcome: Supported graph audio and MKV media render and play through the real media path.
Media fallback and expanded audio remain bounded rather than retaining unbounded whole files.

acceptableVariations: Native codec availability and player chrome may vary for the supported fixture.
nonRequirements: Exact playback controls, waveform styling, or decoder implementation.

contracts.userOutcome: On compact viewports, sidebars behave as modal overlays with safe dismissal.
On roomy viewports, sidebars remain persistent rather than becoming modal overlays.

acceptableVariations: Breakpoint and geometry may evolve, but modal overlay behavior on compact viewports and persistent sidebar behavior on roomy viewports are blocking.
nonRequirements: Exact breakpoint pixel value, drawer width, overlay opacity, or transition duration.

contracts.userOutcome: One app process keeps two graph windows distinct and routes Quick Capture to the intended open graph.

acceptableVariations: Window placement and OS activation order may vary while graph routing remains correct.
nonRequirements: Exact native window coordinates or stacking order.

contracts.userOutcome: A native Linux titlebar close uses Tine's safe persistence path and closes the real window.

acceptableVariations: Window-manager decoration details and button geometry may vary.
nonRequirements: Exact titlebar theme, icon size, or frame border width.

contracts.userOutcome: Slash page and tag references can be authored, selected, rendered, and persisted through the real editor.

acceptableVariations: Completion ranking and popup layout may evolve while explicit reference authoring remains intact.
nonRequirements: Exact popup dimensions, suggestion order for unrelated items, or DOM structure.

contracts.userOutcome: The outline guide toggles collapsible descendants in one accessible action and preserves the parent state.

acceptableVariations: Guide visual treatment and control placement may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact guide-line thickness, color, or animation.

contracts.userOutcome: Page actions reveal or open the exact Markdown or Org source file that backs the visible page.

acceptableVariations: The host opener and menu presentation may vary while the resolved source identity stays exact.
nonRequirements: Exact menu order, iconography, or external application launch behavior.

contracts.userOutcome: Page property actions preserve literal header structure through edit, guarded save, reload, and keyboard traversal.

acceptableVariations: Panel layout and native form-control styling may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact section spacing, control dimensions, or debounce timing.

contracts.userOutcome: The quiet target below page content focuses or creates exactly one writable trailing block.

acceptableVariations: Target affordance styling and scroll position may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact target height, cursor blink timing, or focus-ring style.

contracts.userOutcome: PDF highlights, links, annotations, outline destinations, and restored view state interoperate with Logseq data.
PDF asset switches and target navigation isolate reader state without crashes or runaway resource use.
contracts.authority: Tine PDF interoperability and reader resource-safety contract

acceptableVariations: Reader layout, coordinate normalization, theme presentation, and annotation menu placement may evolve while the persisted interoperability outcomes remain intact.
nonRequirements: Exact drag threshold.
Exact theme palette/RGB.
Exact CSS filter/DOM strategy.

contracts.userOutcome: An in-place graph switch and ordinary safe close persist PDF work only through the window-local graph generation that created it.
contracts.authority: Tine PDF graph-owner generation and safe-close persistence contract

acceptableVariations: PDF rendering and graph-switch presentation may vary while the old graph is drained, the viewer is remounted, and sidecar bytes remain owner-isolated.
nonRequirements: Exact PDF pixels, graph-switch animation, or debounce completion time.

contracts.userOutcome: Signed community plugin and theme metadata remains bound to the reviewed package identity, and executable plugins install disabled until explicitly enabled.
contracts.authority: Tine signed plugin registry and capability-review contract

contracts.userOutcome: Users can inspect safety evidence, install, enable, configure, and uninstall a community plugin or theme on desktop and compact settings layouts.

acceptableVariations: Catalogue card layout, explanatory wording, and settings geometry may evolve while provenance, review state, lifecycle controls, and compact usability remain available.
nonRequirements: Exact screenshot pixels, toast stacking, card dimensions, or animation timing.

contracts.userOutcome: A persisted enabled plugin activates under a verified non-revoking cache, then a verified cached revocation prevents its command or decoration and durably disables it while the live registry endpoint stalls.

acceptableVariations: The reserved signed fixture may use a host-owned command or thread-lines block-decoration contribution, provided the positive control makes activation observable.
nonRequirements: Exact styling, worker implementation details, or live-request timing before the abort deadline.

contracts.userOutcome: Page-menu PDF export creates only a static, script-disabled print frame.

acceptableVariations: Platform print integration and visual page layout may vary after the isolated frame is created.
nonRequirements: Exact print-dialog appearance, paper margins, or font rasterization.

contracts.userOutcome: Generated sites preserve source capability, safe URL handling, contextual anchors, and CSP at the export boundary.
contracts.authority: Tine published-site capability and CSP contract

acceptableVariations: Generated markup organization and browser rendering may vary while the security boundary holds.
nonRequirements: Exact generated whitespace, CSS class names, or visual theme.

contracts.userOutcome: Graph search, query workspaces, references, evidence, filters, and materialization preserve their visible query state.

acceptableVariations: Result presentation and pane layout may evolve while membership, predicates, evidence, and safe materialization remain correct.
nonRequirements: Exact row heights, result-card DOM structure, or tab animation.

contracts.userOutcome: Renaming a page updates the real graph and keeps linked-reference behavior coherent.

acceptableVariations: Navigation timing and rendered reference layout may vary after rename.
nonRequirements: Exact file-list ordering or transition animation.

contracts.userOutcome: Right-sidebar disclosures, bulk controls, edit-safe unmounting, and restart restoration retain their state correctly.

acceptableVariations: Disclosure icons and collection layout may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact panel width, transition duration, or icon artwork.

contracts.userOutcome: Primary panes retain independently usable, theme-aware scrolling affordances.

acceptableVariations: Scrollbar thickness, palette, overlay behavior, and platform rendering may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact scrollbar palette/RGB, thumb dimensions, CSS selector, or DOM structure.

contracts.userOutcome: Ctrl-K search preserves canonical Unicode matching, current-page scope, evidence, and Shift-Enter sidebar opening.

acceptableVariations: Search result layout and ranking among equally valid matches may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact result-row geometry, highlight palette, or animation.

contracts.userOutcome: Real editor selection wrapping preserves Logseq-compatible literal delimiter and Alt-modified behavior.

acceptableVariations: Selection painting and browser key-event timing may vary.
nonRequirements: Exact selection color, caret blink, or editor DOM nesting.

contracts.userOutcome: Sheet cells commit before navigation, arrow keys follow the real event path, and field rename migrates dependent data atomically.

acceptableVariations: Grid dimensions, cell styling, and focus-ring presentation may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact cell pixel size, column widths, or formula-editor layout.

contracts.userOutcome: Favorites and Recent collapse independently, retain per-graph state, and restore correctly.

acceptableVariations: Sidebar ordering and disclosure visuals may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact sidebar width, icon artwork, or transition duration.

contracts.userOutcome: Global Back and Forward preserve the focused split pane's history and promote the user-activated Recent page.

acceptableVariations: Toolbar styling and split geometry may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact button placement, tab width, or transition animation.

contracts.userOutcome: Structured HTML and Markdown paste preserves approved outline structure atomically without unsafe interpretation.
contracts.authority: Tine structured-paste safety and persistence contract

acceptableVariations: Browser clipboard plumbing and rendered formatting may vary while the persisted safe structure is preserved.
nonRequirements: Exact clipboard-event implementation, indentation pixels, or paste animation.

contracts.userOutcome: Overflowing pane tabs expose an accessible overview that activates, closes, and restores focus without unintended drag capture.

acceptableVariations: Tab widths, overflow trigger placement, and overview layout may evolve.
nonRequirements: Exact truncation glyphs, animation, or drag threshold.

contracts.userOutcome: IME-committed Unicode hashtag text keeps bare-tag autocomplete active across editor surfaces.

acceptableVariations: IME composition timing and picker layout may vary by platform.
nonRequirements: Exact candidate ordering for unrelated tags or popup geometry.

contracts.userOutcome: The real GTK/Tauri window advertises Tine's desktop-entry identity on the Wayland wire.

acceptableVariations: Compositor diagnostics and task-switcher presentation may vary.
nonRequirements: Exact task-switcher icon size, title text layout, or compositor animation.

contracts.userOutcome: The real Windows WebView2 app can complete its core smoke workflow with real backend and disk state.

acceptableVariations: Windows chrome, WebView2 startup timing, and native control rendering may vary.
nonRequirements: Exact titlebar palette, DPI-scaled geometry, or startup animation.

contracts.userOutcome: Formula value pickers dismiss an active child before their editor without changing the formula draft.

acceptableVariations: Picker layout and formula-editor presentation may evolve while dismissal ownership remains correct.
nonRequirements: Exact option-row geometry, menu animation, or iconography.
